op.name: frexp
op.notes: Decompose x into mantissa and exponent element-wise.
op.summary: Decompose the elements of x into mantissa and twos exponent.
op.signature: we.frexp(x[, out1, out2], / [, out=(None, None)], *, where=True, casting='same_kind', order='K', dtype=None, subok=True[, signature])

blocks.text: Decompose the elements of x into mantissa and twos exponent.

Returns (`mantissa`, `exponent`), where ``x = mantissa * 2**exponent``.
The mantissa lies in the open interval(-1, 1), while the twos
exponent is a signed integer.

desc_blocks.text: Array of numbers to be decomposed.
Output array for the mantissa. Must have the same shape as `x`.
Output array for the exponent. Must have the same shape as `x`.

items.name: out
items.type: ndarray, None, or tuple of ndarray and None, optional

desc_blocks.text:
A location into which the result is stored. If provided, it must have
a shape that the inputs broadcast to. If not provided or None,
a freshly-allocated array is returned. A tuple (possible only as a
keyword argument) must have length equal to the number of outputs.

This condition is broadcast over the input. At locations where the
condition is True, the `out` array will be set to the ufunc result.
Elsewhere, the `out` array will retain its original value.
Note that if an uninitialized `out` array is created via the default
``out=None``, locations within it where the condition is False will
remain uninitialized.

For other keyword-only arguments, see the
:ref:`ufunc docs <ufuncs.kwargs>`.

Floating values between -1 and 1.
This is a scalar if `x` is a scalar.

Integer exponents of 2.
This is a scalar if `x` is a scalar.

blocks.text: Complex dtypes are not supported, they will raise a TypeError.
ldexp — Compute ``y = x1 * 2**x2``, the inverse of `frexp`.
